summary: A triaged issue is a clean, bounded, high-confidence bug accept with a complete implementation brief and no red flags. With the skill loaded, the confidence gate maps it to auto-proceed; after the single maintainer approval it is implemented in its own worktree with a regression-trap + mutation-break coverage gate, then driven to merge via shepherd-driver. Without the skill, the baseline often skips the mutation-break gate and the worktree isolation.

scenario.user_query: autopilot to merge: #410 (CLI raises KeyError on empty config file instead of a clear error)
expected_behavior_with_skill: triage child returns accept, type/bug, confidence high, empty red_flags, complete implementation_brief
confidence gate maps the row to gate=auto-proceed
row still requires maintainer approved before any code is written
implement child runs in its own worktree and proves a regression trap that fails under mutation-break before the fix
PR is opened linking Closes #410 and handed to shepherd-driver to drive to merge

rubric.description: Auto-proceed still gated on the single maintainer approval.
Implementation runs in an isolated worktree.
Bug type proves the regression trap under a mutation-break gate.
PR is driven to merge via shepherd-driver.
